Title: Melatonin enhances cotton immunity to Verticillium wilt via manipulating lignin and gossypol biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enhances cotton immunity/resistance to Verticillium wilt; promotes downstream lignin and gossypol accumulation during pathogen defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhSNAT1 reduces endogenous melatonin, compromises cotton resistance to Verticillium dahliae, and decreases lignin and gossypol accumulation after infection; exogenous melatonin rescues the phenotype.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhSNAT1 was identified as a cotton homolog of Arabidopsis SNAT1 and functionally validated by complementation of the Arabidopsis snat1 mutant. 2. VIGS silencing of GhSNAT1 reduced melatonin levels in leaves, stems, and roots. 3. GhSNAT1-silenced plants showed higher disease index and fungal biomass after V. dahliae inoculation. 4. GhSNAT1 silencing reduced induction of phenylpropanoid and gossypol-pathway genes, lowering lignin and gossypol accumulation. 5. Exogenous melatonin pretreatment restored resistance and metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homolog identification; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; TRV-VIGS silencing; qPCR/RT-PCR; HPLC-MS melatonin quantification; disease index assay; fungal biomass qPCR; lignin and gossypol measurements; melatonin rescue treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression of the D-subgenome homolog than the A-subgenome homolog in different cotton tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in leaves, stems, and roots; D-subgenome homolog preferentially expressed
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment (25 μM)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for increasing endogenous melatonin to enhance Verticillium wilt resistance; can be combined with external melatonin application or stacking with lignin/gossypol pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes strong disease susceptibility; no growth/yield penalty reported for manipulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats GhSNAT1 as a melatonin biosynthesis gene and upstream regulator of defense-related metabolic reprogramming rather than as a direct lignin/gossypol pathway enzyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enhances cotton immunity/resistance to Verticillium wilt; promotes downstream lignin and gossypol accumulation during pathogen defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhCOMT reduces endogenous melatonin, compromises cotton resistance to Verticillium dahliae, and decreases lignin and gossypol accumulation after infection; exogenous melatonin rescues the phenotype.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCOMT was identified as a cotton homolog of Arabidopsis COMT and functionally validated by complementation of the Arabidopsis comt mutant. 2. VIGS silencing of GhCOMT reduced melatonin levels in leaves, stems, and roots. 3. GhCOMT-silenced plants showed enhanced susceptibility to V. dahliae with increased fungal biomass. 4. GhCOMT silencing reduced induction of phenylpropanoid and gossypol-pathway genes, lowering lignin and gossypol accumulation. 5. Exogenous melatonin pretreatment restored resistance and metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homolog identification; Arabidopsis mutant complementation; TRV-VIGS silencing; qPCR/RT-PCR; HPLC-MS melatonin quantification; disease index assay; fungal biomass qPCR; lignin and gossypol measurements; melatonin rescue treatment
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: caffeic acid O-methyltransferase; dimerization motif (Pfam: pfam08100) and O-methyltransferase motif (Pfam: pfam00891)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression of the D-subgenome homolog than the A-subgenome homolog in different cotton tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in leaves, stems, and roots; D-subgenome homolog preferentially expressed
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment (25 μM)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to raise endogenous melatonin and improve Verticillium wilt resistance; should be considered with caution because COMT also intersects phenylpropanoid/lignin metabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy due to dual association with melatonin biosynthesis and phenylpropanoid methylation, but no explicit growth penalty reported.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper GhCOMT is functionally defined through melatonin complementation and VIGS phenotypes; although COMT enzymes can participate in phenylpropanoid metabolism generally, the focal role here is endogenous melatonin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terpenoid precursor supply branch to sesquiterpene/gossypol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate in the MVA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; described as the key gene for conversion of HMG-CoA to MVA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Melatonin pretreatment upregulates GhHMGR during V. dahliae infection, consistent with increased gossypol accumulation; GhHMGR expression decreases in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among surveyed MVA-pathway genes, GhHMGR was the only one significantly upregulated by melatonin pretreatment at certain infection time points. 2. This upregulation coincided with stronger gossypol accumulation after V. dahliae inoculation. 3. GhHMGR expression was reduced when endogenous melatonin biosynthesis genes were silenced.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qPCR expression analysis in VIGS plants; metabolite profiling of gossypol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after V. dahliae inoculation in melatonin-pretreated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment; GhSNAT1/GhCOMT VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cotton phytoalexin involved in defense against pathogen invasion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream flux-control gene for increasing gossypol-mediated disease resistance, potentially in combination with melatonin pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing gossypol may affect seed use/toxicity, though not discussed directly in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not provide a locus ID for GhHMGR.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for gossypol branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses isoprenoid units to produce farnesyl diphosphate, the sesquiterpene precursor for gossypol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression decreased in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants during infection, consistent with reduced gossypol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFPS was included among known gossypol-related genes whose expression dropped in melatonin-deficient VIGS plants. 2. Reduced GhFPS expression paralleled reduced gossypol accumulation and higher disease susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR expression analysis in VIGS plants; gossypol quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced at 12 hpi in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; melatonin deficiency via GhSNAT1/GhCOMT silencing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cotton phytoalexin involved in pathogen defense
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply engineering target for strengthening gossypol-based defense.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential elevated gossypol-related seed toxicity not discussed in the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not highlighted as a principal target of melatonin in the transcriptome heatmap, but functionally linked through VIGS-associated expression changes.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms branch-specific precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to (+)-δ-cadinene, the committed sesquiterpene intermediate leading to gossypol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Melatonin pretreatment upregulates GhCDNS during infection and GhCDNS expression is reduced in melatonin-deficient VIGS plants, matching lower gossypol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCDNS is one of six critical enzymes highlighted in the gossypol pathway. 2. Its expression was upregulated by melatonin pretreatment after V. dahliae inoculation. 3. Its expression was reduced in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants, coinciding with lower gossypol content and greater susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq heatmap; qPCR expression analysis in VIGS plants; gossypol quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced between 6 and 48 hpi in melatonin-pretreated plants; reduced at 12 hpi in VIGS plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment; GhSNAT1/GhCOMT silencing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cotton phytoalexin involved in defense against pathogen invasion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting gossypol-based resistance; suitable for stacking with upstream precursor-supply genes or melatonin biosynthesis genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher gossypol could have antinutritional/toxicity implications for seed products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion cites prior literature describing CDNS as a key gossypol-pathway enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative modification branch of cadinene-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate on the route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidative step in the cadinene-to-gossypol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by melatonin pretreatment during infection; reduced in melatonin-deficient VIGS plants; associated with increased gossypol accumulation and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP706B1 was one of six critical enzymes highlighted in the gossypol pathway. 2. Transcriptomic induction by melatonin pretreatment was validated by qPCR. 3. Its expression decreased in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants together with lower gossypol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qPCR validation; VIGS-associated expression analysis; gossypol measurement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced between 6 and 48 hpi in melatonin-pretreated plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment; GhSNAT1/GhCOMT silencing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream engineering target to reinforce gossypol branch flux in disease-resistant cotton.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential elevated gossypol-related antinutritional effects not discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact reaction product was not specified in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate on route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidative tailoring step in gossypol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by melatonin pretreatment and reduced in melatonin-deficient plants, consistent with enhanced gossypol accumulation by melatonin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP82D113 was highlighted as a critical gossypol-pathway enzyme. 2. Melatonin-induced expression was supported by qPCR validation. 3. Reduced expression in VIGS plants paralleled lower gossypol levels and higher disease susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qPCR validation; VIGS-associated expression analysis; gossypol measurement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced between 6 and 48 hpi in melatonin-pretreated plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment; GhSNAT1/GhCOMT silencing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream branch target for increasing defensive gossypol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential elevated gossypol-related antinutritional effects not discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact biochemical step not specified in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate on route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidative step in the downstream gossypol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was induced by melatonin pretreatment during infection, consistent with stronger gossypol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP71BE79 was one of the six critical enzymes in the identified gossypol pathway. 2. Its melatonin-induced expression pattern was validated by qPCR. 3. The study inferred stronger downstream branch activation toward gossypol under melatonin treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced between 6 and 48 hpi in melatonin-pretreated plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for reinforcing disease-associated gossypol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential elevated gossypol-related antinutritional effects not discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains a likely typographical inconsistency (GhCYP17BE79 in one sentence); pathway figure and context indicate GhCYP71BE79.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late intermediate on route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a Fe(II)/2-oxoglutarate-dependent oxidation in the downstream gossypol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated by melatonin pretreatment during infection and reduced in melatonin-deficient plants, correlating with gossypol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gh2-ODD-1 was highlighted as one of six critical enzymes in the gossypol pathway. 2. Melatonin-induced expression was confirmed by qPCR. 3. Lower expression in GhSNAT1/GhCOMT-silenced plants accompanied reduced gossypol levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qPCR validation; VIGS-associated expression analysis; gossypol quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced between 6 and 48 hpi in melatonin-pretreated plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: V. dahliae inoculation; exogenous melatonin pretreatment; GhSNAT1/GhCOMT silencing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate late-pathway target to strengthen cotton chemical defense.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential elevated gossypol-related antinutritional effects not discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact substrate/product not specified in this paper.
